message: Stage -1.2 drafted angle=0 v1 mode=cold-start runner=codex/gpt-5.5 status=completed
Filled sharp alpha-profile Lee--Gamma partial-identification derivation for the always-selected effect.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b — boundary stage_0.5_to_0, latest review status=reject with conjecture-level novelty grounds (tier_genuinely_below flagship floor across all three attempts); regression pattern (a) same novelty flag repeating despite producer's revisions. The conjecture as stated is a finite binary-table sensitivity profile that cannot be elevated to flagship without a different angle (named published comparator with proven strict extension or iff frontier). Re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget.
Sharp alpha-profile Lee-Gamma bounds for the always-selected effect with SGJR/JR and no-exclusion compatibility frontiers.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b + regression pattern (a) — both reviews reject on the same novelty axis: the proposal cannot reach the flagship novelty floor because it has no cited open-problem target and its SGJR/JR/Honore-Hu comparators are proposal-defined rather than named published estimators; the producer's second pass even self-downgrades to field tier. This is a conjecture-level promise/tier gap (proposal_promise_gap=kernel_substituted), not a derivation defect another Stage 0 round can fix.
Sharp alpha-profile Lee--Gamma partial-identification note completed for the always-selected effect.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b (Stage 0.5 reject fast-path) — reviewer rejects on novelty floor; artifact itself concedes field-level tier and admits no published open-problem cite for the finite bidirectional (Gamma,kappa) regime, with compat-frontier framed against proposal-defined SGJR/JR and Honore-Hu benchmarks rather than named published estimators. Correctness passes; the defect is conjecture-level (kernel_substituted) — re-deriving on the same .tex would reproduce the same sub-flagship result.
Stage 0 split completed (attempt 1): lee-gamma-frontier=partial, strict-tightening=confirmed, coupling-iff=confirmed

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): case 6b — Stage 0.5 reject is conjecture-level (novelty), not derivation: reviewer explicitly states the proposal targets a field-level sharp bound, concedes it is not a published open-regime claim and not a named-estimator equivalence frontier, and that the flagship-style external-comparator non-representability clause of prop:lee-gamma-frontier-partial is not proved. Regression pattern (a) is moot (single attempt); the kernel itself was substituted (proposal_promise_gap=kernel_substituted), so re-deriving on the same .tex would still fail the orchestrator-enforced flagship floor. Pivot to a new angle instead of burning Stage 0 budget on an admittedly sub-flagship conjecture.
